use serde::Deserialize;
use std::io::{BufRead, BufReader, Read};

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct Record {
    id: u64,
    value: String,
}

fn process_records_streaming(reader: impl Read) -> Result<u64, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let stream = serde_json::Deserializer::from_reader(reader).into_iter::<Record>();
    let mut total: u64 = 0;
    for record in stream {
        let record = record?;
        total += record.id;
    }
    Ok(total)
}

fn count_entries_streaming(reader: impl BufRead) -> Result<usize, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let mut count = 0;
    let stream = serde_json::Deserializer::from_reader(reader).into_iter::<serde_json::Value>();
    for _ in stream {
        count += 1;
    }
    Ok(count)
}

fn process_lines(reader: impl Read) -> Result<Vec<Record>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let buf_reader = BufReader::new(reader);
    let mut records = Vec::new();
    for line in buf_reader.lines() {
        let line = line?;
        let record: Record = serde_json::from_str(&line)?;
        records.push(record);
    }
    Ok(records)
}
